# Phase 10 Adaptation Resilience Plan

Phase: 10.0.32 - Constitutional Civilization Adaptation, Resilience Evolution & Long-Horizon Survivability Doctrine  
Status: Planning-only. Runtime remains disabled.

## Objective

Define how constitutional civilization adapts under radical change while preserving continuity, identity, evidence, dissent, prudence, resilience, and long-horizon legitimacy.

## Scope

- Constitutional adaptation doctrine.
- Resilience evolution framework.
- Anti-fragility governance standards.
- Adaptive survivability philosophy.
- Environmental-change continuity.
- Unknown-risk adaptation.
- Institutional elasticity.
- Long-horizon resilience continuity.
- Adaptive identity preservation.
- Survivability under transformation.
- Constitutional continuity through change.
- Resilience without fragmentation.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- APIs, providers, adapters, credentials, or OAuth.
- Prisma, Supabase, Twilio, email, or background jobs.
- Autonomous adaptation systems.
- Automatic constitutional evolution.
- Self-modifying governance systems.
- Resilience autopilot.
- Repository mutation systems.
- Rollback execution.
- Deployment authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Planning docs remain isolated under governance documentation paths.
- Adaptation preserves identity, evidence, dissent, and reviewability.
- Resilience remains fail-closed under ambiguity.
- Transformation controls do not imply execution authority.
- Runtime and deployment authority remain prohibited.
- Final summary documents audits, risks, and boundaries.
